humanSummary.text: Audit verified that legacy profile writes and profile init now share the same validation and redaction gate, with no P0/P1 blockers. One non-blocking CLI compatibility cleanup is recorded as a follow-up.

findings.title: Legacy profile command compatibility policy
findings.description: The compatibility-only `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` spelling is now safely gated, but the long-term policy should be explicit so future CLI changes do not accidentally preserve or remove the legacy path without intent.

evidence.detail: Final audit found no P0/P1 issues and suggested a future cleanup node to document whether the legacy spelling is supported or deprecated.

findings.impact: CLI behavior is safe today, but future agents may not know whether the legacy command should remain supported, warn, or be removed.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` compatibility policy is documented as supported or deprecated.
CLI tests cover the chosen behavior for the legacy spelling.

proposedDagNode.title: Legacy profile command compatibility policy
proposedDagNode.summary: Define and enforce the compatibility policy for the legacy `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` command path after centralized profile write gating.
deliverables: Documented CLI policy for `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` in docs/subprojects-kaifuu.md
A focused kaifuu-cli test covering either the legacy command warning path or the explicit supported compatibility path
acceptanceCriteria: `kaifuu profile <game-dir>` is intentionally documented as supported or deprecated.
The selected compatibility behavior is covered by a CLI regression test.
auditFocus: Legacy profile command behavior changes without documented policy
